body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall experience in the International Business program at the School of Business Administration during this academic year has been decent, with meaningful highs and some notable lows that I believe the institution has both the capacity and responsibility to address. The academic content, when delivered well, is genuinely rigorous and relevant, and I have formed valuable relationships with peers and some excellent faculty members. However, structural issues — inconsistent teaching quality, administrative inefficiencies, and gaps in student support — have at times undermined what could otherwise be an exceptional educational experience. With targeted improvements and a genuine commitment to acting on student feedback, this institution has the foundations to become truly outstanding.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation services at the School of Business Administration, offered through the Career Center, are decent and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The International Business curriculum at the School of Business Administration covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are fair in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: Extracurricular activities at the School of Business Administration offer a satisfactory range of options that serve the interests of a portion of the student body but do not yet reflect the full diversity of student interests and backgrounds. Core clubs and major university events are reasonably well-organized and attended, but support for emerging student initiatives is limited and the process for establishing new clubs is unnecessarily bureaucratic. Increasing the budget allocated to student activities, streamlining club registration procedures, and investing in purpose-built student activity spaces would encourage broader participation and strengthen the campus culture for the benefit of all enrolled students.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation services at the School of Business Administration, offered through the Career Center, are satisfactory and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services provided by the Finance Department at the School of Business Administration have been outstanding this semester, and I genuinely applaud the clarity, transparency, and efficiency with which they are managed. Fee statements are issued promptly and accurately, with clear breakdowns of tuition, facility charges, and any applicable scholarship deductions that make it easy to verify and plan payments. Payment options are flexible and well-communicated, including installment plans that meaningfully reduce financial pressure on students and families. Scholarship disbursements are processed on time and confirmed through official notifications, removing uncertainty around critical financial support. The finance office staff are professional, patient, and genuinely helpful when navigating complex financial queries.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure at the School of Business Administration, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a fair level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning resources and materials provided for my International Business courses at the School of Business Administration are impressive and represent a significant strength of the program. Course packs are comprehensive, well-organized, and current, incorporating recent academic publications, industry white papers, and multimedia content that enriches the learning experience beyond traditional textbooks. The university's digital library provides broad access to peer-reviewed journals and e-books, and the Learning Management System is intuitive, well-maintained, and effectively used by lecturers to share materials in a timely manner. I also acknowledge the provision of recorded lectures, which allows students to revisit complex material at their own pace — a genuinely valuable academic resource.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: Academic administrative processes at the School of Business Administration are satisfactory in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the International Business student experience at the School of Business Administration center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only satisfactory outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
